Sakaki (Cleyera japonica) is a plant native to Japan, also known as the "Sakaki tree" or "Japanese sakaki". The health benefits of Sakaki include its potential anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may help to alleviate symptoms of colds, fever, and skin irritations. The bioactive constituents of Sakaki include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and alkaloids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional Japanese medicine, Sakaki is often used to make herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and decoctions, as well as being incorporated into traditional Japanese sweets and desserts.
